Document.description: The "document" instance is required to initiate a document. Required properties: "name", "structure". Optional properties: "document_params", "document_style".

name.description: The name to be used when saving the file. A file name should contain .docx extension. A file name can't contain any of the following characters \ / : * ? " < > |

structure.description: The "structure" instance lists all object instances of the "document". The order objects appear in the list reflects the order in the document. Optional instances: "text", "heading", "math_expression", "bullet_list", "table_of_contents", "page_break".

Document.title: Document

Structure.description: The "structure" instance lists all object instances of the "document". The order objects appear in the list reflects the order in the document. Optional instances: "text", "heading", "math_expression", "bullet_list", "table_of_contents" and "page_break".

table_of_contents.title: Table of contents
table_of_contents.description: The "table_of_contents" instance generates a table of contents in the "document" based on all "heading" instances.

Structure.title: Structure

Document Parameters.description: The "document_params" instance lists the page parameters applicable for the "document". Optional properties: "bottom_margin", "left_margin", "right_margin", "top_margin", "landscape", "page_numbering", "page_number_alignment" and  "skip_cover_page".

landscape.description: The "landscape" property specifies if landscape page orientation should be used for the "document".

top_margin.description: The "top_margin" property specifies the top margin for all pages in the "document" in Cm.

bottom_margin.description: The "top_margin" property specifies the bottom margin for all pages in the "document" in Cm.

left_margin.description: The "top_margin" property specifies the left margin for all pages in the "document" in Cm.

right_margin.description: The "top_margin" property specifies the right margin for all pages in the "document" in Cm.

page_numbering.description: The "page_numbering" property specifies if page numbers should be added for the "document".

skip_cover_page.description: The "skip_cover_page" property specifies if cover page should be skipped during page numbering for the "document".

page_number_alignment.description: The "page_number_alignment" property specifies the alignment of page numbering for the "document".

Document Parameters.title: Document Parameters

Document Style.description: The "document_style" instance lists default styles for "text" and "heading" instances of the "document". The default styles will be applied to all corresponding instances within the "document". Every separate instance can still be altered individually via its own properties. Optional instances: "default_text_style" and "default_heading_style".

default_heading_style.description: The "default_heading_style" instance specifies the default style for all "heading" instances within the "document". Every separate "heading" instance can still be altered individually via its own properties. Optional properties: "alignment", "bold", "colour", "first_line_indent", "font", "highlight_color", "italic", "keep_together", "keep_with_next", "left_indent", "level", "line_spacing", "line_spacing_rule", "page_break_before", "right_indent", "size", "space_after", "space_before" and "underline".

Document Style.title: Document Style

Default Text Style.description: The "default_text_style" instance specifies the default style for all "text" instances within the "document". Every separate "text" instance can still be altered individually via its own properties. Optional properties: "alignment", "bold", "colour", "first_line_indent", "font", "highlight_color", "italic", "keep_together", "keep_with_next", "left_indent", "line_spacing", "line_spacing_rule", "page_break_before", "right_indent", "size", "space_after", "space_before" and "underline".

colour.description: The "colour" property specifies the text color for this font. The format can be: RGB (for example '0, 0, 0'); HEX (for example '#ffffff'); standard 8 colours ('black', 'blue', 'green', 'red', 'yellow', 'amber', 'white', 'dark blue', 'magenta', 'cyan').

size.description: The "size" property specifies the font height in English Metric Units (EMU).

bold.description: The "bold" property causes the text of the paragraph to appear in bold face.

italic.description: The "italic" property causes the text of the paragraph to appear in italics.

underline.description: The "underline" property specifies the text underline using true, false or Microsoft Word underline styles.

font.description: The "font" property specifies the text font using Microsoft Word fonts.

left_indent.description: The "left_indent" property specifies the space between the left margin and the left side of the paragraph in Cm.

right_indent.description: The "right_indent" property specifies the space between the right margin and the right side of the paragraph in Cm.

first_line_indent.description: The "first_line_indent" property specifies the relative difference in indentation for the first line of the paragraph. A positive value causes the first line to be indented. A negative value produces a hanging indent.

alignment.description: The "alignment" property specifies the justification setting for this paragraph.

highlight_color.description: The "highlight_color" property specifies the colour of highlighting applied.

space_after.description: The "space_after" property specifies the spacing to appear between this paragraph and the subsequent paragraph in Cm.

space_before.description: The "space_before" property specifies the spacing to appear between this paragraph and the prior paragraph in Cm.

line_spacing_rule.description: The "line_spacing_rule" property specifies how the value of line_spacing should be interpreted. Assigning any of the members like single or double will cause the value of line_spacing to be updated to produce the corresponding line spacing.

line_spacing.description: The "line_spacing" property specifies the space between baselines in successive lines of the paragraph in Pt. The Pt value class is a convenient way to apply line spacing in units of points.

page_break_before.description: The "page_break_before" property specifies if the paragraph should appear at the top of the page following the prior paragraph.

keep_with_next.description: The "keep_with_next" property specifies if the paragraph should be kept on the same page as the subsequent paragraph when the document is rendered. For example, this property could be used to keep a section heading on the same page as its first paragraph.

keep_together.description: The "keep_together" property specifies if the paragraph should be kept "in one piece" and not broken across a page boundary when the document is rendered.

Default Text Style.title: Default Text Style

Text.description: The "text" instance initiates a paragraph in the output document. Required properties: "body". Optional properties: "alignment", "bold", "colour", "first_line_indent", "font", "highlight_color", "italic", "keep_together", "keep_with_next", "left_indent", "line_spacing", "line_spacing_rule", "page_break_before", "right_indent", "size", "space_after", "space_before" and "underline".

body.description: The "body" property specifies the paragraph's text. Separate text parts can be parameterized using \command{text} format, for example \textit{Hello world!}. Parameters can overlap, for example \textbf{\textit{Hello world!}}. For the whole list of available parameterization please check the full documentation.

Text.title: Text

Heading.description: The "heading" instance initiates a paragraph with Heading style applied in the output document. The "level" property specifies the heading's level. Required properties: "body", "level". Optional properties: "alignment", "bold", "colour", "first_line_indent", "font", "highlight_color", "italic", "keep_together", "keep_with_next", "left_indent", "line_spacing", "line_spacing_rule", "page_break_before", "right_indent", "size", "space_after", "space_before" and "underline".

body.description: The "body" property specifies the paragraph's text. Separate text parts can be parameterized using \command{text} format, for example \textit{Hello world!}. Parameters can overlap, for example \textbf{\textit{Hello world!}}. For the whole list of available parameterization please check the full documentation.

Heading.title: Heading

BulletList.description: The "bullet_list" instance initiates a bulleted or numbered list in the output document. Required properties: "bullet_list_style" and "items".

bullet_list_style.description: The "bullet_list_style" property specifies the Microsoft Word style of bulleted list.

items.description: The "items" instance lists all object instances applicable for this bullet list. Optional instances: "text" and "math_expression".
items.title: Items

BulletList.title: BulletList

Items.description: The "items" instance lists all object instances applicable for this bullet list. Optional instances: "text" and "math_expression".
Items.title: Items

MathExpression.description: The "math_expression" instance initiates an equation in the output document. The equation should be specified using LaTeX's features for typesetting mathematics. The "math_expression" provides the display math mode, meaning expressions are not part of a paragraph and put on separate lines. The inline math mode allows to write formulas that are part of a "text" or "heading" instance. For the inline mode the mathematical expressions should be written within ${...}$ delimiters, for example 'This means: ${x^2 + y^2 = z^2}$'. Required properties: "body".

body.description: The "body" property specifies the LaTeX equation.

MathExpression.title: MathExpression

PageBreak.description: The "page_break" instance allows to add blank pages to the output document. Required properties: "number_of_pages".

number_of_pages.description: The "number_of_pages" property specifies the number of subsequent blank pages to be added to the output document.

PageBreak.title: PageBreak
